title: viola tricolor subsp. grandiflora
intro: Viola tricolor subsp. grandiflora, also known as heartsease, is a subspecies of the common violet plant, characterized by its bright colors and delicate appearance. The plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The health benefits of Viola tricolor subsp. grandiflora include its ability to soothe and calm the skin, as well as its potential to reduce stress and anxiety due to its high content of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. Herbal preparations of Viola tricolor subsp. grandiflora include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
